data.title: Google Steps Up Its Long Running Challenge to Nvidia With New AI Chips — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Levine

Ahead of its Google Cloud Next keynote presentation Wednesday afternoon, Alphabet announced new artificial-intelligence chips, the eighth generation of Google's TPU. Google uses these chips to bring down its own cost of serving AI, and it also rents out the servers built around them in the cloud, most notably to AI startup Anthropic.

Alphabet shares were up 1.7% in early trading.

Google was an early investor in AI, and in the mid-2010s it began to run into the same problem many others saw in recent years. Nvidia's GPU chips and software, the standard kit for AI research since 2012, were expensive and hard to come by in the large quantities that Google required. To rectify that, it embarked on what is now a long journey to make its own chips that could do the same tasks, but more efficiently. The first-generation TPU was released in 2015 and Google has been producing new and better versions of the chips ever since.

Broadcom has long been Google's chip-design partner.

Fast-forward to 2026 and non-GPU AI chips have become more common. Google Cloud's biggest competitors, Amazon Web Services and Microsoft's Azure, both have their own custom AI chips. Nvidia just made a $20 billion licensing deal with an AI-chip startup called Groq and will be jointly produce servers later this year. Another AI-chip startup, Cerebras, just submitted its initial public offering prospectus to the Securities and Exchange Commission last week.

There are two versions of TPU 8 and the server clusters that are built around them. One is for training AI models, like Google's Gemini language models, and the other is for running those models to power applications like the Gemini chatbot, or the company's Jules agent that writes software code.

Like Nvidia has recently, Google's announcement was less about the new chips and more about the hardware that is built around them, something Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang calls "extreme co-design". Much of Google's technical "deep-dive" on TPU 8 was about how Google eliminated bottlenecks in networking between servers in a cluster so they can better work as one.

The servers are orchestrated by Google's own Axion CPUs instead of comparable chips from Intel or Advanced Micro Devices. Axion is based on technology from Arm, which is another boost to Arm's ambitions in the data center.

The TPU 8 servers will be available on Google Cloud later this year.

Write to Adam Levine at adam.levine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Genvor Sets $800,000 Convertible Financing and Equity-Funded Advisory Deal to Back Listing Plans
data.description:
Genvor announced two material agreements to bolster liquidity and advance a potential national exchange listing. The company arranged a securities purchase agreement with Evergreen Capital Management for an $800,000 convertible note and up to 600,000 warrants, providing up to $666,668 in cash proceeds in four tranches, with later tranches conditioned on filing a Form S-1 and receiving SEC comments. The 10% note matures in nine months from the issue date or upon listing and is convertible at $1.00 per share, or at 80% of the lowest 5-day VWAP upon default, subject to a 4.99% cap. Genvor also engaged Brio Advisory Group under an equity-based advisory structure valued at $300,000 per tranche upon listing, or convertible into common stock if no listing occurs within one year.

Agreement 1: Genvor Arranges $800,000 Convertible Note Financing With Evergreen; $666,668 Purchase Price

  • Agreement type: Securities purchase agreement for up to $800,000 convertible note and 600,000 warrants
  • Counterparty: Evergreen Capital Management
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / Apr 16 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Note: 9 months or until exchange listing; Warrants: 5 years
  • Reason: Provide funding and support planned exchange listing

Agreement 2: Genvor Engages Brio Advisory Group With Equity-Based Compensation Tied to Exchange Listing

  • Agreement type: Advisory agreement for strategic, financing, and listing support
  • Counterparty: Brio Advisory Group
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / Apr 16 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Advance strategic initiatives and prepare for an exchange listing

Original SEC Filing: Genvor Inc [ GNVR ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Kopin Shares Rise on $3.2M Initial Contract for Drone Goggles
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Kopin rose after the company received a $3.2 million initial contract to support its first-person-view goggle system for drone pilots.

The stock was up 11% to $3.15 as of midday Wednesday. Shares have gained 35% this year.

Kopin said the contract marks its formal entry into the FPV drone market. The optical systems company said the contract draws on its new Sentinel FPV concept for drone pilots, which includes OLED microdisplays in a monocular or binocular configuration, and is designed to allow pilots to maintain awareness of their immediate environment.

The company's initial contract will cover the delivery of up to 40,000 goggles through 2028, with additional follow-on potential. Kopin did not name the buyer.

Kopin is seeking additional partners to integrate the Sentinel FPV technology, it said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: LNG containment firm GTT's Q1 revenue rises on strong orders
data.description:
Overview

  • France membrane containment tech firm's Q1 2026 revenue rose 1% yr/yr

  • Company recorded 32 orders in Q1 2026, double the prior year

  • Middle East conflict has not directly impacted GTT’s business activity to date

Outlook

  • GTT confirms 2026 revenue guidance of EUR 740 mln to EUR 780 mln

  • Company expects 2026 EBITDA between EUR 490 mln and EUR 530 mln

Result Drivers

  • ORDER GROWTH - Co recorded 32 orders in Q1 2026, including LNG carriers and ethane carriers, marking its second-best first-quarter commercial performance

  • CONTAINMENT SYSTEMS SLOWDOWN - Revenue from containment systems fell due to fewer vessels under construction, following the end of a prior order peak

  • MARINE DIVISION EXPANSION - GTT Marine division revenue grew, driven by Danelec acquisition and new contracts for hardware and software solutions

data.title: ICE Canola Makes Gains Along With Crude Oil
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were showing modest gains in the middle of Wednesday trading, receiving ongoing support from crude and vegetable oils.

Despite United States President Donald Trump announcing an extended ceasefire with Iran on Tuesday, Iranian forces attacked three vessels on the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, seizing two of them. As a result, crude oil advanced by more than US$2 per barrel.

Chicago soyoil was higher, as well as European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil.

The Canadian dollar was down one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared with Tuesday's close.

About 39,400 canola contracts have traded at 11:13 a.m. EDT. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton:

 
           Price      Change 
May       724.80     up 3.80 
Jul       738.30     up 3.30 
Nov       734.50     up 4.50 
Jan       740.70     up 3.70

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Orange Polska Q1 net profit jumps 55%, confirms 2026 outlook
data.description:
Orange Polska , the Polish arm of French telecoms group Orange , on Wednesday maintained its full-year guidance for low single-digit percentage growth in both revenue and core profit while posting better-than expected first-quarter results.

Orange Polska is Poland's largest telecoms operator, where it competes with players such as Cyfrowy Polsat .

  • "Taking into account the results achieved in the first quarter, we are convinced that despite the demanding and unstable market environment, we will achieve our full-year goals", CEO Liudmila Climoc said in a press release

  • The company reported a first-quarter net profit of 295 million zlotys ($81 million), up 55% year-on-year and above analysts' expectations for a profit of 253 million zlotys

  • The beat was driven by core earnings growth and real estate sales

  • The company's core profit (EBITDAaL) stood at 913 million zlotys, compared with a forecast of 879 million

  • Revenue of 3.18 billion zlotys came in below a forecast of 3.20 billion zlotys

($1 = 3.6206 zlotys)

data.title: Navigate Health Drives +48% Ad Revenue Growth with Opti Digital
data.description:
The digital health platform strengthens monetisation and drives revenue growth while preserving performance and user trust.

PARIS, FR / ACCESS Newswire / April 22, 2026 / Opti Digital, the leading AdTech company specialising in publisher revenue optimisation, today announced a successful collaboration with Navigate Health through its Ad Manager Hub.

Navigate Health is a UK-based digital health company dedicated to making healthcare more accessible, personalised, and human. At the core of its ecosystem is Patient.info, a trusted online health resource providing reliable medical content to millions of users worldwide, including both the public and healthcare professionals.

As a premium publisher operating in a highly sensitive content environment, Navigate Health sought to increase advertising revenue while maintaining full control, transparency, and a high-quality user experience essential to audience trust. At the same time, the company aimed to simplify increasingly complex monetisation operations and refocus internal resources on strategic growth.

Navigate Health sought a partner capable of driving immediate revenue impact while preserving the integrity of its user experience and improving visibility over its monetisation strategy.

"Opti Digital has enabled us to unlock meaningful revenue growth while maintaining the high-quality user experience that is core to Patient.info. Their team quickly became a true support, bringing both the technology and strategic expertise needed to scale with confidence," said Thomas Andrew Porteus, Director at Navigate Health.

Immediate Performance Gains Through Faster Ad Delivery

To support these objectives, Opti Digital deployed its Ad Manager Hub, designed to unlock revenue growth while optimising performance and maintaining long-term audience value.

From the moment the solution was implemented, Navigate Health experienced a significant uplift in performance driven by faster and more efficient ad delivery.

The lightweight wrapper immediately improved ad server response times: the share of fast calls (under 2 seconds) increased from 5% to 35%, while slow calls (over 4 seconds) dropped from 64% to 33%.

This acceleration translated into stronger viewability, more efficient ad rendering, and a smoother user experience, contributing to improved Core Web Vitals and sustained revenue growth.

Unified Data and Smarter Decision-Making

Through Opti Digital's Insights Hub, Navigate Health consolidated audience and monetisation data into a single, real-time decision layer.

By connecting user behaviour with revenue performance, the team was able to better understand profitability across traffic sources and content types, optimise page layouts, and align editorial strategy with monetisation outcomes.

This unified approach enabled faster, more informed decision-making while strengthening overall yield performance.

Increased Competition and High-Impact Formats

To further maximise revenue, Opti Digital introduced a continuous test-and-learn approach to demand optimisation, increasing bid competition through advanced supply-path optimisation designed to shape traffic, maximise bid density, and improve auction efficiency.

At the same time, high-performing formats and placements were deployed, including interstitial ads and dynamic ad insertion strategies. These placements adapt to content structure and length, ensuring optimal ad density while maintaining a seamless user experience.

Advanced Monetisation Optimisation

Navigate Health also leveraged advanced monetisation capabilities within the Opti Digital platform.

Smart In-View Ad Refresh allowed placements to refresh only when actively visible, ensuring additional impressions were generated from engaged sessions without compromising viewability or user experience.

Combined with AI-powered dynamic floor pricing, these optimisations maximised competition and ensured each impression was monetised at its highest potential value.

Importantly, these improvements not only drove programmatic performance but also supported the delivery of high-performing direct campaigns through enhanced viewability and efficient ad delivery.

Driving Sustainable Growth

Following implementation, Navigate Health achieved a +48% increase in ad revenue and a 380% return on investment, alongside significantly faster ad delivery performance.

"The impact was immediate, from faster ad delivery to increased yield," added Thomas Andrew Porteus. "We now have greater visibility and control over our monetisation, allowing us to focus on long-term growth rather than day-to-day optimisation."

This collaboration highlights how premium publishers in sensitive verticals such as health can unlock meaningful revenue growth while maintaining strict standards for user experience, transparency, and trust.

"Sustainable monetisation today requires balancing performance, user experience, and data-driven decision-making," said Magali Quentel-Reme, CEO of Opti Digital. "Our role is to help publishers like Navigate Health achieve that balance while maximising the value of their inventory."

About Opti Digital

Opti Digital is a leading AdTech company helping digital publishers maximise revenue and connecting brands with premium audiences. Through its monetisation suite, proprietary demand, and advanced analytics, Opti Digital enables publishers to optimise advertising performance while delivering high-impact campaigns for brands.

More than 10,000 websites and apps worldwide, including publishers such as Euronews, Gizmodo, LADbible, Valnet, and Ziff Davis, among other publishers, rely on Opti Digital to drive measurable results and long-term growth.

Media Contact

Sara Bigovic

Communications & Events Manager, Opti Digital

sara@optidigital.com

www.optidigital.com

SOURCE: Opti Digital

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Kongsberg Maritime ASA – Completion of the demerger of Kongsberg Gruppen ASA
data.description:
22 April 2026 – Reference is made to the stock exchange announcements published by Kongsberg Gruppen ASA ("KOG ASA") on 30 October 2025, 17 December 2025, 22 January 2026 and 16 April 2026, regarding the demerger and separate listing of the Kongsberg Maritime business area on Euronext Oslo Børs.

Kongsberg Maritime ASA ("KM ASA") is pleased to inform that the necessary registrations in the Norwegian Register of Business Enterprises to complete the demerger have been made, and that the demerger has been completed.

As a result of completion of the demerger:

  • KM ASA has issued in total 879,609,245 new shares (the "Demerger Consideration Shares"). The Demerger Consideration Shares will accrue to those who are shareholders in KOG ASA as of today, 22 April 2026 (as registered in KOG ASA's register of shareholders with Euronext Securities Oslo on 24 April 2026). The Demerger Consideration Shares will be tradable from 23 April 2026.
  • KM ASA's new share capital is NOK 52,776,554.70 divided into 879,609,245 shares, each with a nominal value of NOK 0.06.

The shares in KM ASA will tomorrow, 23 April 2026, commence trading on Euronext Oslo Børs under the ticker "KMAR". The reference price for the KM ASA share on Euronext Oslo Børs on 23 April 2026 will be set at NOK 67.00, which implies a reference price for the KOG ASA share of NOK 307.20 on the same date (based on the closing trading price on Euronext Oslo Børs for KOG ASA's shares on 22 April 2026). The actual opening trading prices for each of KM ASA and KOG ASA will be determined based on Euronext Oslo Børs’ call auction mechanism considering the order book development until 09:00 tomorrow morning. The reference price for the KM ASA share of NOK 67.00 has been determined based on an update of the underlying valuation for the division ratio in the demerger that was carried out in connection with the signing of the demerger plan in December 2025 reflecting developments in e.g. order book, financial position, financial estimates, market developments, etc. since then.

About Us

Kongsberg Maritime is a global leader in maritime technology

We deliver advanced solutions to merchant, offshore, fishing, and naval vessels, setting new standards for safe, efficient, and sustainable operations - both for new builds and existing fleets. In close collaboration with customers and research environments, we develop technology that solves real challenges at sea.

Today, over 30,000 vessels operate with our systems on board.

Kongsberg Maritime has over 8,300 employees in 35 countries.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section 5 -12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act and Euronext Oslo Rule Book II - Issuer Rules, at 2026-04-22 17:47 CEST.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical Shares Rise After 1Q Beat
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Intuitive Surgical shares advanced after the company's adjusted earnings and revenue beat analysts' expectations.

Shares of the robotic surgery systems company rose 8.2% on Wednesday to $488.50. For the year, the stock is still down 14%.

The company reported first-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.50 a share after the close on Tuesday. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $2.12 a share. Revenue was $2.77 billion, ahead of the $2.62 billion analysts expected, according to FactSet.

U.S. procedures increased 15%, with notable increases in cholecystectomys and appendectomys, Chief Financial Officer Jamie Samath said during the earnings call. Outside the U.S. total procedures grew 20%, reflecting strong results in India, Canada, the United Kingdom, South Korea and Taiwan, despite challenges in China, Samath said.

The company expects to expand the role of artificial intelligence in its procedures, such as AI assisting surgeons with identifying parts of the body, Chief Executive David Rosa said.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com
